---
title: "Link between Experiences and Cognition multioutput approach"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r libraries}
library(tidyverse)
library(catboost)
library(caret)
library(latex2exp)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(igraph)
library(cowplot)
library(gridExtra)
library(scales)
```

```{r load_data}

label_dict <- list(
  # Demographics
  "age_at_test" = "age",

  # Global Ecology
  "pct_Interacting"     = "P[int]",
  "pct_Not_Interacting" = "P[paste('\u00AC', int)]",

  # Vocal Activity
  "kcs_per_overall"         = "KCS[all]",
  "kcs_per_Interacting"     = "KCS[int]",
  "kcs_per_Not_Interacting" = "KCS[paste('\u00AC',int)]",

  # Speech Exposure (total)
  "total_speech_exposure_per_overall"         = "Exps[all]",
  "total_speech_exposure_per_Interacting"     = "Exps[int]",
  "total_speech_exposure_per_Not_Interacting" = "Exps[paste('\u00AC', int)]",

  # Child-directed speech
  "kcds_exposure_per_overall"         = "KCDS[all]",
  "kcds_exposure_per_Interacting"     = "KCDS[int]",
  "kcds_exposure_per_Not_Interacting" = "KCDS[paste('\u00AC', int)]",

  # Overheard speech
  "ohs_exposure_per_overall"         = "OHS[all]",
  "ohs_exposure_per_Interacting"     = "OHS[int]",
  "ohs_exposure_per_Not_Interacting" = "OHS[paste('\u00AC', int)]",

  # Turn-Taking
  "turns_per_minute_overall"     = "TpM[all]",
  "turns_per_minute_interacting" = "TpM[int]",
  "agency_initiation_prop"       = "P[agency]",
  "avg_turn_duration"            = "bar(D)[turn]",
  "avg_block_duration"           = "bar(D)[block]",
  "avg_turns_per_block"          = "bar(N)[paste(turn, '/', block)]",

  # Visuals
  "pct_face_visible"           = "P[face]",
  "pct_person_visible"         = "P[person]",
  "pct_face_or_person_visible" = "P[paste(face, '/', person)]",
  "avg_face_proximity"         = "bar(d)[face]"
)

label_vec <- unlist(label_dict)

# Load Data
df <- read.csv("../data/final_feature_df.csv")

df$orev_prop <- df$orev_successes / df$orev_trials 
df$prag_prop <- df$prag_successes / df$prag_trials

target_names <- c("orev_prop", "prag_prop")

# STEP 2: Define columns to exclude from predictors (IVs)
exclude_cols <- c(
  "child_id", "sex", "age_at_recording", "min_age_rec",
  "orev_successes", "prag_successes", "age_at_test",
  "orev_trials", "prag_trials", "tango_trials", 
  "tango_error", "orev_prop", "prag_prop"
)

# IVs are everything in the dataframe EXCEPT the excluded list 
iv_list <- setdiff(names(df), exclude_cols)

min_real_age <- round(min(df$age_at_test),2)
max_real_age <- round(max(df$age_at_test), 2)

# Load the comprehensive hyperparameter grid log to verify model metrics later
orev_tuning_log <- read.csv("../outputs/orev_hyperparameter_tuning_log.csv")
prag_tuning_log <- read.csv("../outputs/prag_hyperparameter_tuning_log.csv")

# Load target sheets directly from local files
orev_raw_shap <- read.csv("../outputs/orev_prop_final_oof_shap_values.csv") %>%
  rename(child_id = child_idx, Feature = feature_raw, ActualValue = feature_value, 
         DirectionalShap = shap_value, FeatureLabel = feature_paper_label) %>%
  group_by(Feature) %>%
  mutate(FeatureLevel = (ActualValue - min(ActualValue)) / (max(ActualValue) - min(ActualValue))) %>%
  ungroup()

prag_raw_shap <- read.csv("../outputs/prag_prop_final_oof_shap_values.csv") %>%
  rename(child_id = child_idx, Feature = feature_raw, ActualValue = feature_value, 
         DirectionalShap = shap_value, FeatureLabel = feature_paper_label) %>%
  group_by(Feature) %>%
  mutate(FeatureLevel = (ActualValue - min(ActualValue)) / (max(ActualValue) - min(ActualValue))) %>%
  ungroup()
```

```{r remove_multicollinearity}
# =====================================================================
# STEP 4: SUBGRAPH MEDOID FEATURE SELECTION (Removes Multicollinearity)
# =====================================================================
cat("--- Starting Subgraph Medoid Feature Selection ---\n")

# 1. Calculate Correlation Matrix on all initial IVs
X_all_ivs <- df %>% select(all_of(iv_list))
X_numeric <- X_all_ivs %>% select(where(is.numeric))
cor_matrix_before <- cor(X_numeric, use = "pairwise.complete.obs", method = "pearson")

# save correlation matrix 
write.csv(cor_matrix_before, file = "../outputs/correlation_matrix_before.csv", row.names = TRUE)

dist_matrix <- 1 - abs(cor_matrix_before)

# 2. Create Adjacency Matrix (Threshold: absolute r >= 0.75 means distance <= 0.25)
threshold <- 0.25
adj_matrix <- ifelse(dist_matrix <= threshold, 1, 0)
diag(adj_matrix) <- 0 # Remove self-loops

# 3. Create Graph and find Maximal Cliques (Highly correlated groups)
g <- igraph::graph_from_adjacency_matrix(adj_matrix, mode = "undirected")
cliques <- igraph::max_cliques(g, min = 2)

assigned_features <- c()
selected_medoids <- c()

# 4. Loop through groups and find the Medoid
for (i in seq_along(cliques)) {
  clique_features <- names(cliques[[i]])
  unassigned_features <- setdiff(clique_features, assigned_features)
  
  if (length(unassigned_features) > 1) {
    # Find the feature with the highest average correlation to others in this specific group
    sub_cor <- abs(cor_matrix_before[unassigned_features, unassigned_features])
    avg_cor <- colMeans(sub_cor)
    medoid <- names(which.max(avg_cor))
    
    selected_medoids <- c(selected_medoids, medoid)
    assigned_features <- c(assigned_features, unassigned_features)
    
    cat(sprintf("\nGroup %d found:\n  Features: %s\n  -> Selected Captain (Medoid): %s\n", 
                i, paste(unassigned_features, collapse = ", "), medoid))
  }
}

# 5. Keep independent features + selected medoids
independent_features <- setdiff(iv_list, assigned_features)
iv_list_clean <- c("age_at_test",independent_features, selected_medoids)

cat(sprintf("\nReduced feature set from %d to %d (Preserving SHAP interpretability!)\n", 
            length(iv_list), length(iv_list_clean)))
cat("--------------------------------------------------\n")

# 6. Update iv_list and X to only include the surviving features
iv_list <- iv_list_clean
X <- df %>% select(all_of(iv_list))
# =====================================================================

# 7. Prepare targets
Y <- df %>% select(orev_prop, prag_prop)

# 8. Ensure data is clean (Remove NAs from targets)
clean_indices <- which(complete.cases(df[, target_names]))
df_clean <- df[clean_indices, ]
X_clean <- X[clean_indices, , drop = FALSE]
Y_clean <- Y[clean_indices, , drop = FALSE]
child_ids_clean <- df$child_id[clean_indices]
```

```{r visualize_correlation_matrix}
# 1. Compute Matrix After Selection
X_after <- X_clean %>% select(where(is.numeric))
names(X_after)
cor_matrix_after <- cor(X_after, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

# 2. Upgraded Plotting Function
# Upgraded Plotting Function showing only significance stars
# Clean Plotting Function (Colors only, no text/stars)
plot_cor <- function(m, title) {
  # Get the original variable ordering from the matrix rows/columns
  var_order <- colnames(m)
  
  # Hide the upper triangle AND the diagonal
  m[upper.tri(m, diag = TRUE)] <- NA
  
  # Convert matrix to a long data frame
  df_plot <- as.data.frame(as.table(m))
  names(df_plot) <- c("V1", "V2", "r")
  
  # Remove empty rows
  df_plot <- subset(df_plot, !is.na(r))
  
  # Trim the factor levels so empty outer rows/columns disappear
  x_levels <- var_order[-1]
  y_levels <- var_order[-length(var_order)]
  
  df_plot$V1 <- factor(df_plot$V1, levels = x_levels)
  df_plot$V2 <- factor(df_plot$V2, levels = y_levels)
  
  # Filter out any lingering outside data
  df_plot <- subset(df_plot, !is.na(V1) & !is.na(V2))
  
  # HELPER FUNCTION: Looks up the plotmath string and parses it into an expression
  label_parser <- function(breaks) {
    mapped_strings <- ifelse(breaks %in% names(label_vec), label_vec[breaks], breaks)
    parse(text = mapped_strings)
  }
  
  ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = V1, y = V2, fill = r)) +
    geom_tile(color = "white", size = 0.4) + 
    
    # FORCE A PERFECT 1:1 SQUARE ASPECT RATIO
    coord_fixed() + 
    
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#0571b0", high = "#ca0020", mid = "#f7f7f7", 
                         limit = c(-1,1), breaks = c(-1, -0.5, 0, 0.5, 1.0)) +
    
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = title, x = "", y = "") + 
    
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 24, angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, color = "black"),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 24, color = "black"),
      panel.grid = element_blank(), 
      
      # REMOVE ALL INTERNAL MARGIN PADDING
      plot.margin = margin(0, 0, 0, 0, "pt"),
      
      legend.position = c(0.15, 0.75), 
      legend.direction = "vertical",
      legend.background = element_blank(),
      legend.title = element_blank(), 
      legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
      legend.key.height = unit(1.5, "cm"),
      legend.key.width = unit(1, "cm")
    ) +
    
    scale_x_discrete(labels = label_parser, drop = TRUE) +
    scale_y_discrete(labels = label_parser, drop = TRUE)
}

# 3. Display Side-by-Side
plot_before <- plot_cor(cor_matrix_before, "Correlation matrix before feature selection")
plot_after <- plot_cor(cor_matrix_after, "")

# 5. Save them directly to your computer
ggsave("../outputs/correlation_matrix_before.png", plot = plot_before, width = 8, height = 8, dpi = 600, scale=0.8)
ggsave("../outputs/correlation_matrix_after.png", plot = plot_after, width = 12, height = 12, dpi = 600, scale=0.7)
saveRDS(plot_after, "../outputs/correlation_matrix_after.rds")
```

```{r evaluate_model_separated, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# =====================================================================
# STEP 5: TARGET-SEPARATED BOOTSTRAP METRIC CRUNCHER
# =====================================================================
# 2. Extract baseline training metric constants from winning configurations
orev_train_r2   <-0.3311
orev_train_rmse <- 0.1202
orev_train_mae <- 0.0982

prag_train_r2   <- 0.8549
prag_train_rmse <- 0.0573
prag_train_mae <- 0.0330

# 2. Extract a clean, deduplicated vector of actual vs predicted values per child
# Since the SHAP file repeats child entries for every feature, we grab just 1 feature row per child
extract_clean_predictions <- function(shap_dataframe) {
  shap_dataframe %>%
    filter(Feature == "age_at_test") %>% # Keeps exactly 1 row per child_id
    select(child_id, actual_value, predicted_value) %>%
    distinct(child_id, .keep_all = TRUE)
}

orev_predictions_df <- extract_clean_predictions(orev_raw_shap)
prag_predictions_df <- extract_clean_predictions(prag_raw_shap)

# 3. Core function to calculate standard performance indices per sample group
calculate_base_metrics <- function(actual, pred) {
  rmse_val <- sqrt(mean((actual - pred)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
  mae_val  <- mean(abs(actual - pred), na.rm = TRUE)
  
  mean_act <- mean(actual, na.rm = TRUE)
  r2_val   <- 1 - (sum((actual - pred)^2, na.rm = TRUE) / sum((actual - mean_act)^2, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  # Suppress warnings in case constant vectors occur during resampling iterations
  spearman_val <- suppressWarnings(cor(actual, pred, method = "spearman"))
  
  return(c(R2 = r2_val, MAE = mae_val, RMSE = rmse_val, Spearman = spearman_val))
}

# 4. Engine to execute non-parametric bootstrapping with true child-id resampling
bootstrap_target_metrics <- function(predictions_df, iterations = 2000) {
  set.seed(123) # Guarantee reproducibility for manuscript writing
  n_samples <- nrow(predictions_df)
  
  # Compute true original empirical values
  empirical <- calculate_base_metrics(predictions_df$actual_value, predictions_df$predicted_value)
  
  # Run bootstrap loops
  boot_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow = iterations, ncol = 4)
  colnames(boot_matrix) <- c("R2", "MAE", "RMSE", "Spearman")
  
  for (b in 1:iterations) {
    sampled_indices <- sample(1:n_samples, n_samples, replace = TRUE)
    boot_sample     <- predictions_df[sampled_indices, ]
    
    boot_matrix[b, ] <- calculate_base_metrics(
      boot_sample$actual_value, 
      boot_sample$predicted_value
    )
  }
  
  # Extract exact 2.5th and 97.5th percentiles for the 95% Confidence Intervals
  ci_lower <- apply(boot_matrix, 2, function(x) quantile(x, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE))
  ci_upper <- apply(boot_matrix, 2, function(x) quantile(x, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  # Consolidate into a clean flat list object for inline R Markdown use
  result_list <- list(
    R2             = empirical["R2"],
    R2_lower       = ci_lower["R2"],
    R2_upper       = ci_upper["R2"],
    
    MAE            = empirical["MAE"],
    MAE_lower      = ci_lower["MAE"],
    MAE_upper      = ci_upper["MAE"],
    
    RMSE           = empirical["RMSE"],
    RMSE_lower     = ci_lower["RMSE"],
    RMSE_upper     = ci_upper["RMSE"],
    
    Spearman       = empirical["Spearman"],
    Spearman_lower = ci_lower["Spearman"],
    Spearman_upper = ci_upper["Spearman"]
  )
  
  return(result_list)
}

# 5. Run evaluation loops independently for both targets using the extracted tables
orev_metrics <- bootstrap_target_metrics(orev_predictions_df, iterations = 2000)
prag_metrics <- bootstrap_target_metrics(prag_predictions_df, iterations = 2000)

# Append internal training constants directly onto objects for inline formatting
orev_metrics$Train_R2   <- orev_train_r2
orev_metrics$Train_RMSE <- orev_train_rmse
orev_metrics$Train_MAE <- orev_train_mae
prag_metrics$Train_R2   <- prag_train_r2
prag_metrics$Train_RMSE <- prag_train_rmse
prag_metrics$Train_MAE <- prag_train_mae

# 7. Save standalone consolidated csv metrics sheets to your machine
metric_summary_table <- data.frame(
  Domain          = c("OREV_Vocabulary", "PRAG_Pragmatics"),
  
  # Out-of-Fold Test R2 Bounds
  OOF_R2          = c(orev_metrics$R2, prag_metrics$R2),
  OOF_R2_Lower    = c(orev_metrics$R2_lower, prag_metrics$R2_lower),
  OOF_R2_Upper    = c(orev_metrics$R2_upper, prag_metrics$R2_upper),
  
  # Out-of-Fold Test MAE Bounds
  OOF_MAE         = c(orev_metrics$MAE, prag_metrics$MAE),
  OOF_MAE_Lower   = c(orev_metrics$MAE_lower, prag_metrics$MAE_lower),
  OOF_MAE_Upper   = c(orev_metrics$MAE_upper, prag_metrics$MAE_upper),
  
  # Out-of-Fold Test RMSE Bounds
  OOF_RMSE        = c(orev_metrics$RMSE, prag_metrics$RMSE),
  OOF_RMSE_Lower  = c(orev_metrics$RMSE_lower, prag_metrics$RMSE_lower),
  OOF_RMSE_Upper  = c(orev_metrics$RMSE_upper, prag_metrics$RMSE_upper),
  
  # Out-of-Fold Spearman Rank Tracking Bounds
  Spearman_Rho    = c(orev_metrics$Spearman, prag_metrics$Spearman),
  Spearman_Lower  = c(orev_metrics$Spearman_lower, prag_metrics$Spearman_lower),
  Spearman_Upper  = c(orev_metrics$Spearman_upper, prag_metrics$Spearman_upper),
  
  # Internal Baseline Training Context (Overfitting Check)
  Train_R2        = c(orev_metrics$Train_R2, prag_metrics$Train_R2),
  Train_RMSE      = c(orev_metrics$Train_RMSE, prag_metrics$Train_RMSE),
  Train_MAE       = c(orev_metrics$Train_MAE, prag_metrics$Train_MAE)
)

write.csv(metric_summary_table, "../outputs/catboost_performance_metrics.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r global_absolute_shap_importance}
# Function to isolate domain and render standard global bar graph
plot_global_shap <- function(shap_df) {
  importance_df <- shap_df %>%
    group_by(Feature, FeatureLabel) %>%
    summarise(Importance = mean(abs(DirectionalShap)), .groups = "drop") %>%
    arrange(desc(Importance))
  
  ggplot(importance_df, aes(x = reorder(FeatureLabel, Importance), y = Importance)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = rgb(128/255, 134/255, 123/255)) +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) parse(text = x)) +
    labs(title = NULL, x = "Predictors", y = expression(bar(abs(SHAP)))) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 40),
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 24),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 24),
      axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 20),
      axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 20),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_blank()
    )
}

# Generate and save separate bar charts
orev_absolute_shap <- plot_global_shap(orev_raw_shap)
prag_absolute_shap <- plot_global_shap(prag_raw_shap)

ggsave("../outputs/orev_mean_shap_importance.png", plot = orev_bar, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 1200) #scale = 0.8)
ggsave("../outputs/prag_mean_shap_importance.png", plot = prag_bar, width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 1200) #scale = 0.8)
```

```{r directional_shap_plot}
# =====================================================================
# PLOT 3 & 4: SEPARATE DIRECTIONAL BEESWARM CHARTS
# =====================================================================
plot_directional_beeswarm_augmented <- function(shap_df, 
                                              threshold = 0.013, 
                                              edge_max_L = 0.060, 
                                              edge_max_R = 0.080, 
                                              buffer = 0.005) {
  # =====================================================================
  # 1. MATHEMATICAL PROJECTIONS USING INSTANCE-SPECIFIC PARAMETERS
  # =====================================================================
  plot_df <- shap_df %>%
    group_by(Feature) %>%
    filter(max(abs(DirectionalShap)) > 0.0001) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(
      PlotX = case_when(
        # Far Left: Project points from -threshold down to -edge_max_L
        DirectionalShap < -threshold ~ -threshold - buffer * (abs(DirectionalShap) - threshold) / (edge_max_L - threshold),
        # Far Right: Project points from +threshold up to +edge_max_R
        DirectionalShap > threshold  ~ threshold + buffer * (DirectionalShap - threshold) / (edge_max_R - threshold),
        # Central area stays perfectly linear
        TRUE ~ DirectionalShap
      )
    )

  # Determine dynamic positions for the white background mask boxes
  mask_left_min  <- -threshold - 0.0005
  mask_left_max  <- -threshold + 0.0005
  mask_right_min <- threshold - 0.0005
  mask_right_max <- threshold + 0.0005

  # =====================================================================
  # 2. GENERATE GRAPHIC WITH DYNAMIC ANNOTATIONS
  # =====================================================================
  ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = PlotX, y = reorder(FeatureLabel, abs(DirectionalShap)))) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "darkgray", linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_jitter(aes(color = FeatureLevel), height = 0.2, width = 0, alpha = 0.7, size = 1.8) +
    
    # Dynamic Opaque Mask Boxes to clear background grid channels at the threshold split points
    geom_rect(xmin = mask_left_min, xmax = mask_left_max, ymin = 0.4, ymax = 13.6, fill = "white", color = NA) +
    geom_rect(xmin = mask_right_min, xmax = mask_right_max, ymin = 0.4, ymax = 13.6, fill = "white", color = NA) +
    
    # Parallel Break Bars (Centered dynamically at ±threshold)
    annotate("segment", x = -threshold - 0.0004, xend = -threshold, y = 0.4, yend = 13.6, color = "darkgray", linewidth = 0.6) +
    annotate("segment", x = -threshold, xend = -threshold + 0.0004, y = 0.4, yend = 13.6, color = "darkgray", linewidth = 0.6) +
    annotate("segment", x = threshold - 0.0004, xend = threshold, y = 0.4, yend = 13.6, color = "darkgray", linewidth = 0.6) +
    annotate("segment", x = threshold, xend = threshold + 0.0004, y = 0.4, yend = 13.6, color = "darkgray", linewidth = 0.6) +
    
    # -----------------------------------------------------------------
    # SPECIFY BREAKS AND LABELS
    # -----------------------------------------------------------------
    scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = c(
        -threshold - 0.005,  # Left Tail Window Tick
        -threshold,          # Central Linear Boundary Left
        0,                   # Center
        threshold,           # Central Linear Boundary Right
        threshold + 0.005    # Right Tail Window Tick
      ),
      labels = c(
        sprintf("-%.3f", edge_max_L),
        sprintf("-%.3f", threshold),
        "0.000",
        sprintf("%.3f", threshold),
        sprintf("%.3f", edge_max_R)
      )
    ) +
    scale_color_gradientn(
      colors = c("#4c72b0", "#c44e52"),
      breaks = c(0.05, 0.95),
      labels = c("Low", "High"),
      limits = c(0, 1)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = NULL, 
      x = paste0("Directional SHAP Value (Axis Breaks at ±", sprintf("%.3f", threshold), ")"), 
      y = "Predictors", 
      color = "Predictor\nValue"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    scale_y_discrete(labels = function(x) parse(text = x)) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 40),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 20),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 20),
      axis.title.x  = element_text(size = 24),
      axis.title.y = element_text(size = 24),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = c(0.9, 0.23),
      legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "lightgray", linewidth = 0.3),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 6)),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 16),
      legend.ticks = element_blank(),
      legend.key = element_blank()
    ) +
    guides(color = guide_colorbar(title.hjust = 0.5, barheight = unit(3.5, "cm"), barwidth = unit(0.25, "cm")))
}

plot_directional_beeswarm <- function(shap_df) {
  
  # 1. Define your exact list of social predictors (excluding age and visuals)
  social_features <- c(
    #"age_at_test", 
    "kcs_per_overall","kcs_per_Interacting",                      
    "total_speech_exposure_per_Interacting", "total_speech_exposure_per_Not_Interacting", "kcds_exposure_per_Interacting",
    "kcds_exposure_per_Not_Interacting", "ohs_exposure_per_Interacting", "turns_per_minute_interacting",
    "agency_initiation_prop", "avg_turn_duration", "pct_face_or_person_visible", "pct_Not_Interacting")
  
  # 2. Filter data frame for social features only, and ensure it contains active variances
  plot_df <- shap_df %>%
    #filter(Feature %in% social_features) %>%
    group_by(Feature) %>%
    filter(max(abs(DirectionalShap)) > 0.0001) %>%
    ungroup()

  # 3. Generate the un-broken, linear visualization
  ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = DirectionalShap, y = reorder(FeatureLabel, abs(DirectionalShap)))) +
    # Reference baseline at exactly zero impact
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "darkgray", linetype = "dashed") +
    
    # Raw individual observations mapped with subtle horizontal jittering
    geom_jitter(aes(color = FeatureLevel), height = 0.2, width = 0, alpha = 0.7, size = 1.8) +
    
    # Let R dynamically generate clean, linear breaks across the actual SHAP ranges
    scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_number(accuracy = 0.001)) +
    
    # Unified color ramp (Low = Blue, High = Red)
    scale_color_gradientn(
      colors = c("#4c72b0", "#c44e52"), 
      breaks = c(0.05, 0.95),
      labels = c("Low", "High"), 
      limits = c(0, 1)
    ) +
    
    # Structured title configurations
    labs(
      title = NULL, 
      x = "Directional SHAP Value", 
      y = "Predictors", 
      color = "Predictor\nValue"
    ) +
    
    theme_minimal() +
    
    # Safely parses your custom plotmath/latex math strings on the Y-axis
    scale_y_discrete(labels = function(x) parse(text = x)) +
    
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 20),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 20),
      axis.title  = element_text(size = 24),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      
      # Clean legend box formatting placement
      legend.position = c(0.9, 0.23),
      legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "lightgray", linewidth = 0.3),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 6)),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 16),
      legend.ticks = element_blank(),
      legend.key = element_blank()
    ) +
    
    guides(
      color = guide_colorbar(
        title.hjust = 0.5, 
        barheight = unit(3.5, "cm"), 
        barwidth = unit(0.25, "cm")
      )
    )
}

orev_beeswarm <- plot_directional_beeswarm(orev_raw_shap)
prag_beeswarm <- plot_directional_beeswarm(prag_raw_shap)

# Render separate beeswarm graphs
orev_aug_beeswarm <- plot_directional_beeswarm_augmented(
  shap_df     = orev_raw_shap, 
  threshold   = 0.013, 
  edge_max_L  = 0.05, 
  edge_max_R  = 0.05
)

prag_aug_beeswarm <- plot_directional_beeswarm_augmented(
  shap_df     = prag_raw_shap, 
  threshold   = 0.05,   # Higher threshold to allow a wider middle linear zone
  edge_max_L  = 0.01,   # Adjusted left axis limit
  edge_max_R  = 0.01    # Adjusted right axis limit
)

ggsave("../outputs/orev_directional_shap_importance.png", plot = orev_beeswarm, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 1200) #scale = 0.8)
ggsave("../outputs/prag_directional_shap_importance.png", plot = prag_beeswarm, width = 16, height = 8, dpi = 1200) #scale = 0.8)
```

```{r dependence_shap_plot}
# =====================================================================
# PLOTS 5-8: SEPARATE VISUAL CAPABILITY PLOT HOOKS (FLAT & INTERACTION)
# =====================================================================

# 1. UPGRADED UNIVERSAL SUB-PLOT RENDERING ENGINE
create_dependence_plot <- function(df, target_feature, color_feature = NULL, display_name, remove_y_axis = FALSE, remove_y_title = FALSE, y_limits = NULL) {
  feature_data <- df %>% filter(Feature == target_feature)
  
  clean_proportion_labels <- function(x) {
    labels <- sprintf("%.2f", x)
    labels[x == 0] <- "0"
    labels[x == 1] <- "1"
    return(labels)
  }
  
  # Map custom math expressions securely
  x_axis_expression <- switch(
    target_feature,
    "kcds_exposure_per_Interacting" = latex2exp::TeX("$KCDS_{int}$"),
    "ohs_exposure_per_Interacting"  = latex2exp::TeX("$OHS_{int}$"),
    "pct_face_or_person_visible"    = latex2exp::TeX("$P_{face/person}$"),
    "turns_per_minute_interacting"  = latex2exp::TeX("$TpM_{int}$"),
    "total_speech_exposure_per_Interacting" = latex2exp::TeX("$Exps_{int}$"), 
    latex2exp::TeX(paste(display_name))
  )
  
  percentage_features <- c(
    "kcds_exposure_per_Interacting", 
    "ohs_exposure_per_Interacting", 
    "pct_face_or_person_visible", 
    "total_speech_exposure_per_Interacting"
  )
  
  p <- ggplot(feature_data, aes(x = ActualValue, y = DirectionalShap))
  
  p <- p + 
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "darkgray", linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_smooth(method = "gam", color = "black", linetype = "solid", size = 1.0, se = FALSE)
  
  if (!is.null(color_feature)) {
    p <- p + 
      geom_point(aes(color = !!sym(color_feature)), alpha = 0.7, size = 2.2) +
      scale_color_gradientn(colors = c("#4c72b0", "#c44e52")) +
      labs(color = "Age \n(Years)")
  } else {
    p <- p + 
      geom_point(color = rgb(128/255, 134/255, 123/255), alpha = 0.6, size = 2.2)
  }
  
  if (target_feature %in% percentage_features) {
    p <- p + scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1))
  } else {
    p <- p + scale_x_continuous()
  }
  
  p <- p +
    labs(title = NULL, x = x_axis_expression, y = "Directional \n SHAP Value") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 20),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 20),
      axis.title  = element_text(size = 24),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.ticks = element_blank(),
      legend.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", margin = margin(b = 6)),
      legend.text  = element_text(size = 16)
    )
  
  if (!is.null(y_limits)) { p <- p + scale_y_continuous(limits = y_limits) }
  
  # New condition: removes ONLY the label text, leaves numbers and ticks
  if (remove_y_title) {
    p <- p + theme(axis.title.y = element_blank())
  }
  
  # Original condition: removes everything on the y-axis
  if (remove_y_axis) {
    p <- p + theme(axis.title.y = element_blank(), axis.text.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.y = element_blank())
  }
  
  return(p)
}


create_flat_age_plot <- function(df, y_limits = NULL) {
  age_data <- df %>% filter(Feature == "age_at_test")
  
  p <- ggplot(age_data, aes(x = ActualValue, y = DirectionalShap)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "darkgray", linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_point(color = rgb(128/255, 134/255, 123/255), alpha = 0.6, size = 2.2) +
    geom_smooth(method = "gam", color = "black", linetype = "solid", size = 1.0, se = FALSE)
  
  # Apply limits directly within scale definition to avoid conflicts
  if (!is.null(y_limits)) { 
    p <- p + scale_y_continuous(limits = y_limits) 
  } else {
    p <- p + scale_y_continuous()
  }
  
  p <- p +
    labs(title = NULL, x = "Age (Years)", y = "Directional \n SHAP Value") +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 20),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 20),
      axis.title  = element_text(size = 24),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
  
  return(p)
}

# 2. HELPER PIPELINE TO EXTRACT AGE MATRIX MAPPINGS
prepare_interaction_df <- function(target_shap_df) {
  
  # 1. Isolate the TRUE unscaled chronological age for each child (ActualValue)
  age_mapping <- target_shap_df %>%
    filter(Feature == "age_at_test") %>%
    select(child_id, AgeValue = ActualValue) %>% # FIXED: Tracks real years, not 0-1 scale
    distinct(child_id, .keep_all = TRUE)
  
  # 2. Join it back and clean out the duplicate columns to avoid confusion
  cleaned_df <- target_shap_df %>%
    left_join(age_mapping, by = "child_id") %>%
    select(
      child_id, target, Feature, FeatureLevel, 
      ActualValue, DirectionalShap, AgeValue,
      baseline_value, predicted_value
    )
  
  return(cleaned_df)
}

orev_interaction_df <- prepare_interaction_df(orev_raw_shap)
prag_interaction_df <- prepare_interaction_df(prag_raw_shap)

# =====================================================================
# 3. COMPILE OREV FIGURES (TOP TWO SOCIAL PREDICTORS)
# =====================================================================
shared_bounds_orev <- c(-0.015, 0.015)

orev_age_bounds <- c(-0.06, 0.06)
prag_age_bounds <- c(-0.12, 0.12)
orev_age_flat_plot <- create_flat_age_plot(orev_interaction_df)
orev_age_flat_plot  <- create_flat_age_plot(orev_interaction_df, y_limits = orev_age_bounds)
prag_age_flat_plot  <- create_flat_age_plot(prag_interaction_df, y_limits = prag_age_bounds)

# Variation 1: Standard Flat Layout (No age colors)
orev_flat_1 <- create_dependence_plot(orev_interaction_df, "ohs_exposure_per_Interacting", color_feature = NULL, "OHS_int", remove_y_axis = TRUE, y_limits = shared_bounds_orev)
orev_flat_2 <- create_dependence_plot(orev_interaction_df, "pct_face_or_person_visible", color_feature = NULL, "P_face/person", remove_y_axis = TRUE, y_limits = shared_bounds_orev)
orev_flat_3 <- create_dependence_plot(orev_interaction_df, "kcds_exposure_per_Interacting", color_feature = NULL, "KCDS_int", remove_y_axis = TRUE, y_limits = shared_bounds_orev)

orev_int_1 <- create_dependence_plot(orev_interaction_df, "ohs_exposure_per_Interacting", color_feature = "AgeValue", "OHS_int", remove_y_axis = FALSE, remove_y_title = TRUE, y_limits = shared_bounds_orev) + theme(legend.position = "none")
orev_int_2 <- create_dependence_plot(orev_interaction_df, "pct_face_or_person_visible", color_feature = "AgeValue", "P_face/person", remove_y_axis = TRUE, y_limits = shared_bounds_orev) + theme(legend.position = "none")
orev_int_3 <- create_dependence_plot(orev_interaction_df, "kcds_exposure_per_Interacting", color_feature = "AgeValue", "KCDS_int", remove_y_axis = TRUE, y_limits = shared_bounds_orev)

# =====================================================================
# 4. COMPILE PRAG FIGURES (TOP TWO SOCIAL PREDICTORS)
# =====================================================================
shared_bounds_prag <- c(-0.04, 0.04)

# Variation 1: Standard Flat Layout (No age colors)
prag_flat_1 <- create_dependence_plot(prag_interaction_df, "kcds_exposure_per_Interacting", color_feature = NULL, "KCDS_int", remove_y_axis = FALSE, y_limits = shared_bounds_prag)
prag_flat_2 <- create_dependence_plot(prag_interaction_df, "turns_per_minute_interacting", color_feature = NULL, "TpM_int", remove_y_axis = TRUE, y_limits = shared_bounds_prag)
prag_flat_3 <- create_dependence_plot(prag_interaction_df, "total_speech_exposure_per_Interacting", color_feature = NULL, "Exps_int", remove_y_axis = TRUE, y_limits = shared_bounds_prag)

prag_int_1 <- create_dependence_plot(prag_interaction_df, "kcds_exposure_per_Interacting", color_feature = "AgeValue", "KCDS_int", remove_y_axis = FALSE, remove_y_title = TRUE, y_limits = shared_bounds_prag) + theme(legend.position = "none")
prag_int_2 <- create_dependence_plot(prag_interaction_df, "turns_per_minute_interacting", color_feature = "AgeValue", "TpM_int", remove_y_axis = TRUE, y_limits = shared_bounds_prag) + theme(legend.position = "none")
prag_int_3 <- create_dependence_plot(prag_interaction_df, "total_speech_exposure_per_Interacting", color_feature = "AgeValue", "Exps_int", remove_y_axis = TRUE, y_limits = shared_bounds_prag)

prag_int_3 <- create_dependence_plot(prag_interaction_df, 
  "total_speech_exposure_per_Interacting", color_feature = "AgeValue", "Exps_int", 
  remove_y_axis = TRUE, y_limits = shared_bounds_prag) + 
  # Overwrite the continuous x-axis breaks to step by 0.20
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1),
    breaks = seq(0, 1, by = 0.20)
  )
```

```{r generate_combined_plot_orev}
master_theme <- theme_minimal(base_size = 24) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 40),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 20),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 24, margin = margin(t = 10)),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 24, margin = margin(t = 10)),
    strip.text = element_blank(),
    strip.background = element_blank(),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    panel.border = element_blank()
  )

orev_absolute_shap_plot <- orev_absolute_shap + master_theme

orev_top_row <- plot_grid(
  orev_absolute_shap_plot,
  orev_age_flat_plot,
  ncol = 2, 
  labels = c("A", "B"), 
  label_fontface = "bold",
  rel_widths = c(1.5, 1))

orev_middle_row <- plot_grid(
  orev_aug_beeswarm_plot,
  ncol = 1,
  labels = c("C"), 
  label_fontface = "bold")

orev_bottom_row <- plot_grid(
  orev_int_1,
  orev_int_2,
  orev_int_3,
  ncol = 3,
  labels = c("C", "D", "E"), 
  label_fontface = "bold",
  align = "h",
  rel_widths = c(1, 0.75, 0.85))

orev_combined <- plot_grid(
  orev_top_row,
  #orev_middle_row,
  orev_bottom_row,
  ncol = 1,
  rel_heights = c(1, 1)) # Allocation: 20% top, 40% middle, 40% bottom
  #rel_heights = c(1.1, 1.1, 0.7)) # Allocation: 20% top, 40% middle, 40% bottom

ggsave("../outputs/orev_shap_combined.png", plot = orev_combined, width = 18, height = 12, dpi = 600, scale = 0.8)
```

```{r generate_combined_plot_prag}
prag_absolute_shap_plot <- prag_absolute_shap + master_theme

prag_top_row <- plot_grid(
  prag_absolute_shap_plot,
  prag_age_flat_plot,
  ncol = 2, 
  labels = c("F", "G"), 
  label_fontface = "bold",
  rel_widths = c(1.5, 1))

prag_bottom_row <- plot_grid(
  prag_int_1,
  prag_int_2,
  prag_int_3,
  ncol = 3,
  labels = c("H", "I", "J"), 
  label_fontface = "bold",
  align = "h",
  rel_widths = c(1, 0.75, 0.85))

prag_combined <- plot_grid(
  prag_top_row,
  prag_bottom_row,
  ncol = 1,
  rel_heights = c(1, 1))

ggsave("../outputs/prag_shap_combined.png", plot = prag_combined, width = 18, height = 12, dpi = 600, scale = 0.8)
```

```{r generate-one-shap-plot}
orev_row <- plot_grid(
  orev_absolute_shap_plot,
  orev_age_flat_plot,
  orev_int_1,
  orev_int_2,
  orev_int_3,
  ncol = 5, 
  align = "h",
  labels = c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E"), 
  label_fontface = "bold",
  rel_widths = c(1.2, 1, 0.95, 0.8, 0.9))

prag_row <- plot_grid(
  prag_absolute_shap_plot,
  prag_age_flat_plot,
  prag_int_1,
  prag_int_2,
  prag_int_3,
  ncol = 5, 
  labels = c("F", "G", "H", "I", "J"), 
  label_fontface = "bold",
  rel_widths = c(1.2, 1, 0.95, 0.8, 0.9))


shap_combined <- plot_grid(
  orev_row,
  prag_row,
  ncol = 1)

ggsave("../outputs/combined_shap_plot.png", plot = shap_combined, width = 24, height = 14, dpi = 600, scale = 0.8)
```
